div.head: HO–5. Editorial Note
div.p: On February 9, 1959, Honduran rebels, under the direction of Armando Velásquez Cerrato, the former Chief of Staff of the Honduran Armed Forces, initiated a revolution against the government of President Villeda Morales and invaded the Honduran province of Santa Barbara. Reacting to the attack, Honduran authorities dispatched elements of the Honduran Army to Santa Barbara and ended the rebellion. Documentation on the incident is in Department of State file 615.17 and 715.00.
